There yet remains in the water


Six persons killed, seven severe
ly injured and many others badly
shaken up 10 the score of terri
BILE wreck which appened shortly
after 4 o'clock yesterday afternoon
near the western portal of tunnel
No. II of the Great Northern,
which is short distance east of
Camden and on the short Of Dia-
mond lake. The train left Newport
10 minutes late and is said to have
been running at high speed when
it left the track.


The cause of the accident Is un.
known and will probably remain
a mystery, because the engineer,
Ed Munson, and the fireman, Frank
Bell, both Of Hillyard. went down
with their locomotive into the deep
waters Of the lake. 1t is believed
that spreading rails threw the en-
CINE of.


A passenger on No. I, which
stopped at the scene Of the wreck
this mornin, says that while there
he heard that some small detached
rock threw the engine from the
track.


One of the operatives said:


There is no use of saying that
the train was travelling at too high
rate Of speed. If the speed of 40
miles an hour was being made,
why, We have schedules calling for
that time on half a dozen trains.
AS to reoklessuess, anyone who
knew Munson and appreciated the
fact that he had for years travelled
that very track, will not suggest
It,"


Nothing new has been received
at the local Offices since last night
except that the track was cleared
at midnight and trains are running
In a short time the master me
chanic's department will commence
the work of raising the engine ant
